content: Shibboleth SP3 shibboleth2.xml and its independent config pieces (metadata, attribute-map, and RequestMapper when split into a separate ReloadableConfiguration file) are file-monitored and reload in a background thread on change, without a shibd restart. However this is per-piece, not guaranteed for every element, and remote/signed-metadata reload has its own failure modes (mandatory XML signing, backup-file corruption on fetch failure can block startup). Practice: validate config with shibd -check before rolling; do not assume every change is hot-reloaded — verify empirically which changes need a pod roll vs a reload signal.
